concreteSteps:
Goal 1: Self-Harm Prevention (Achieved): Maintain safety checks, reinforce their crisis plan, and don't pull back support too quickly just because they're stable.
Goal 2: Coping Skills Development: Teach grounding techniques, breathing exercises, and journaling. Practice during calm moments so they're usable during crisis. Include physical outlets like walks or exercise.
Goal 3: Substance & Alcohol Abuse Recovery: Continue therapy and education on recovery. Increase activities and encourage engagement in hobbies, outings, and social interactions to keep them busy and preoccupied.
Goal 4: Personal Hygiene & Clean Living Space: Daily checklist in their room. Staff prompts that gradually fade as habits build. Weekly room checks framed positively, not punitively.
Goal 5: Counseling for Behavioral Issues: Keep therapy sessions consistent. Staff document behavioral incidents with context so the therapist has real data to work with between sessions.
Goal 6: Independent Living Skills: Focus on one skill per week — cooking, budgeting, transit, appointments. Real-world practice beats classroom teaching.
Goal 7: Anxiety & Depression Management: Medication compliance, daily routine with structure, physical activity, and teaching them to recognize early warning signs of a dip.
Goal 8: Sleep Hygiene: Consistent sleep/wake times, no screens before bed, limit caffeine after noon, and a wind-down routine. Flag persistent issues to the prescriber.
Goal 9: Adjustment to BHRF Program (Achieved): Keep reinforcing structure and start introducing discharge planning conversations so the transition out feels as supported as the transition in.
progressIndicators:
Goal 1: Self-Harm Prevention
No incidents reported, resident voluntarily uses crisis plan, and can verbally identify coping strategies when asked.
Goal 2: Coping Skills Development
Resident uses techniques independently without staff prompting. Fewer emotional escalations over time. Staff observe them applying skills in real situations.
Goal 3: Substance & Alcohol Abuse Recovery
Consistent attendance in therapy and activities. Resident voluntarily participates rather than needing encouragement. Clean drug screens if applicable. Positive peer interactions.
Goal 4: Personal Hygiene & Clean Living Space
Fewer staff prompts needed over time. Room passes weekly checks consistently. Resident maintains routine without reminders.
Goal 5: Counseling for Behavioral Issues
Decrease in documented behavioral incidents. Resident can articulate what triggered an episode and what they could do differently. Therapist reports engagement and progress.
Goal 6: Independent Living Skills
Resident completes tasks with less supervision each week. Can demonstrate skills without guidance — cooking a meal, making an appointment, managing a basic budget.
Goal 7: Anxiety & Depression Management
Self-reported mood improvements. Takes medication consistently without reminders. Maintains daily routine. Engages socially rather than isolating. Prescriber confirms stable presentation.
Goal 8: Sleep Hygiene
Resident falls asleep and wakes at consistent times. Fewer complaints about fatigue or insomnia. Staff observe adherence to the wind-down routine.
Goal 9: Adjustment to BHRF Program
Follows house rules with minimal redirection. Positive relationships with staff and peers. Actively participates in programming and engages in discharge planning conversations.
medicalIssues: Resident reports that she does not need prescribed medication anymore.
plan: Monitor closed and report any observations to provider.
